authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital regulation for medical-record services, prompt completion, authentication, retention, and record content. It directly constrains healthcare record completion and deficiency handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record completion, authentication, retention, and medical-record service governance

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.508 - Uses and disclosures for which an authorization is required
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Binding HIPAA authorization rule for disclosures that require authorization. It directly governs hard refusal when an ROI request lacks proper release authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Individuals' Right under HIPAA to Access their Health Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA regulator. The guidance defines designated-record-set access, exclusions, personal-representative handling, written denial boundaries, and licensed-review escalation for safety-sensitive access denials.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: designated-record-set access and controlled disclosure handling

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI uses and disclosures to the necessary scope. It constrains release and external request handling for non-treatment documentation requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, disclosure limitation, and privacy-sensitive routing

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.526 - Amendment of protected health information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Binding HIPAA amendment rule for amendment requests, denial grounds, append-or-link handling, statements of disagreement, and amendment distribution. It prevents administrative rewriting of records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: amendment request intake, routing, and record-integrity escalation
used_for: refusal of clinical rewriting or deletion

authoritative_sources.title: Complying With Medical Record Documentation Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS MLN guidance for Medicare documentation sufficiency, CERT review, insufficient documentation errors, missing signatures, missing orders, and records needed to support services billed or reviewed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: coding-support, billing-support, audit-support, and documentation sufficiency checks
used_for: incomplete or insufficient documentation failure modes
coding and billing prerequisite handling without coding authority

authoritative_sources.title: Complying With Medicare Signature Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS guidance for signature, attestation, signature log, and authentication evidence. It directly supports unsigned-record deficiency scenarios and audit-readiness checks.

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Record Maintenance & Access Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS provider guidance for maintaining, retaining, furnishing, and granting access to medical records. It grounds retrieval, maintenance, and audit readiness for provider documentation operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record maintenance, retrieval, retention, and furnishing workflow
used_for: external request log and evidence packet structure

authoritative_sources.title: Fundamentals of the Legal Health Record and Designated Record Set
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHIMA is the recognized HIM professional body. The practice brief gives vendor-neutral workflow guidance for legal health record and designated record set definitions, source systems, external records, retention matrices, disclosure readiness, and policy ownership.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legal-health-record, designated-record-set, source-system, retention, and disclosure packet definition
used_for: legal health record and designated record set distinction
source-system, retention matrix, and external-record handling

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability data baseline. It grounds EHR data and document terminology such as clinical notes, encounter information, patient demographics, provenance, and health IT artifact classes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: EHR artifact taxonomy, terminology normalization, and source traceability

workflow_stages.name: Documentation request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify request purpose, patient or member, requester authority, encounter or service dates, jurisdiction, care setting, payer or plan, record-owner context, ROI, amendment, coding-support, billing-support, audit, or deficiency workflow posture.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Record-set and system-of-truth validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether the requested artifacts are part of the tenant legal health record, designated record set, EHR documentation record, HIM deficiency queue, ROI log, document repository, coding-support packet, or external request log; escalate when source precedence is unresolved.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completeness, signature, attestation, consent, and deficiency review
workflow_stages.description: Check encounter notes, consents, orders, addenda, amendments, attestations, signatures, signature logs, external attachments, and deficiency status for administrative completeness without authoring or changing clinical content.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: ROI, access, amendment, retention, and publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Prepare bounded routing notes and evidence packets for ROI, access, amendment, retention, legal-hold, and publication requests only when authorization, legal basis, access constraints, and minimum-necessary posture are known; otherwise escalate.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, conflict, and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing authorization or referral dependencies, PHI-sensitive releases, conflicting records, legal-hold conflicts, payer-policy conflicts, clinical-safety concerns, and requests for clinical, coding, billing, legal, privacy, or payer interpretation.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Encounter notes, progress notes, discharge summaries, procedure notes, orders, and addenda
Consents, authorizations, advance directives when retained in the record, and personal-representative or proxy records
Release-of-information requests, access-request records, disclosure logs, and external request logs
Amendment or correction requests, statements of disagreement, rebuttals, and append-or-link amendment references
Documentation deficiency queue items, record completion audits, incomplete-note flags, and attestation status
Signature evidence, signature logs, provider attestations, and authentication metadata
Legal health record and designated record set definitions, retention matrices, and source-system maps
Document management index entries, scanned outside records, external attachments, and HIM packet manifests
Coding-support, billing-support, payer audit, referral, authorization, and eligibility dependency records
Legal-hold, subpoena, court-order, privacy escalation, and information-blocking or access-exception notes
systems_of_record: Release-of-information workflow system and disclosure log
Record-completion, deficiency, attestation, and signature tracking queues
Patient identity, registration, eligibility, and encounter management systems
Coding, billing, claim-support, payer audit, and revenue-cycle work queues
Referral, authorization, utilization, and payer portal systems when documentation depends on them
Legal hold, compliance, privacy, and audit logging systems
Health information exchange, lab, imaging, or external-source repositories when records are referenced

allowed: Classify documentation requests, validate prerequisite facts, and identify whether the work is in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked.

Determine whether the administrative record set is complete enough for release preparation, coding support, billing support, audit support, or owner follow-up, without approving those downstream outcomes.
Prepare record-completeness summaries, ROI or amendment routing notes, evidence packets, record update notes, and audit-trail or exception summaries.
Route missing signatures, attestations, consents, amendments, deficiencies, legal holds, access constraints, and source conflicts to the correct owner.
Apply stricter tenant policies when they narrow the public baseline and record assumptions.
escalate: Authorization, legal basis, personal representative authority, minimum-necessary scope, consent, legal hold, or access constraint is unknown or disputed.
Jurisdiction, payer, plan, care setting, referral, authorization, eligibility, service date, record owner, or tenant source-of-truth hierarchy is missing and would change handling.
EHR, HIM, ROI, document management, coding, billing, payer, referral, eligibility, or audit systems conflict.
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, facility policy, legal instruction, or jurisdictional rule materially conflict.

A request requires licensed clinical judgment, clinical safety review, privacy-officer decision, legal interpretation, payer-policy interpretation, coding decision, billing decision, or claim adjudication.
refuse: Requests to add, rewrite, infer, backdate, delete, conceal, or clinically interpret official healthcare documentation.
Requests to disclose records without valid authorization, legal basis, requester authority, role-based access, or minimum-necessary support.
Requests to approve clinical content, diagnosis, treatment plan, medical necessity, coding level, claim readiness, payer appeal, legal sufficiency, release approval, or final amendment outcome.
Requests to certify a disputed or incomplete record set as complete when required artifacts, signatures, attestations, consents, amendments, or source precedence remain unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: Treating healthcare documentation as generic document administration and omitting EHR status, HIM deficiency, legal health record, designated record set, authorization, PHI, and source-system constraints.
Releasing or preparing records for disclosure without valid authorization, legal basis, requester authority, personal-representative proof, or minimum-necessary scope.
Marking a coding, billing, payer audit, or claim-support packet ready when required notes, orders, signatures, attestations, or attachments are incomplete.
Using administrative documentation maintenance to rewrite clinical content or decide amendment, medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment, coding, claim, payer, privacy, or legal outcomes.
Failing to preserve append-or-link amendment lineage, statements of disagreement, rebuttal records, addendum dates, or audit trail evidence.
Certifying completeness despite conflicting EHR, HIM, ROI, document management, coding, billing, or external request records.
Ignoring authorization, referral, eligibility, payer, service setting, or care-pathway dependencies that materially affect documentation handling.
Handling PHI-sensitive, legal-hold, information-blocking, or patient-safety-sensitive access decisions without privacy, legal, or licensed clinical escalation.
Applying tenant policy in a way that weakens federal HIPAA, CMS, or HIM baseline constraints instead of escalating the conflict.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
A requested release lacks valid authorization or minimum-necessary support.
Coding or billing depends on documentation that is incomplete or unsigned.
A record amendment request conflicts with record-integrity rules.
Unauthorized or unsupported record change request.
Safe escalation when patient safety, clinical judgment, or licensed review is required.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific retention, release, minor-consent, personal-representative, subpoena, and legal-hold rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Jurisdictional rules can materially change ROI, amendment, retention, access, and legal-hold routing.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction-specific tenant policy or legal-owner retrieval before final release, retention, amendment, or legal-hold statements.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific legal health record and designated record set definitions
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Healthcare organizations define record-set scope, source systems, external-record inclusion, and retention matrices differently.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat the tenant legal health record, designated record set, source-system map, and retention matrix as required facts before certifying completeness or release readiness.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer, plan, authorization, referral, and coverage documentation dependencies
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: CMS guidance does not resolve commercial, Medicaid, managed-care, or contract-specific documentation dependencies.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require payer or plan context and route payer-policy interpretation, authorization approval, and coverage decisions to adjacent payer or revenue-cycle owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Sensitive-record segmentation and patient-safety-sensitive access concerns
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Sensitive records, harm concerns, and information-blocking exception posture can require privacy, legal, and licensed clinical review.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate privacy, legal, and licensed-review questions instead of deciding access denial or release exceptions inside the documentation lane.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because each variable is converted into a required tenant retrieval fact, refusal trigger, or orchestrator escalation.

Research constrains the lane to administrative record integrity, evidence traceability, and routing support; it does not permit clinical content authorship, privacy-law ruling, payer interpretation, coding, billing, or legal outcome decisions.
